summary: Vault Credentials Feature - CRITICAL BUG FIX VERIFIED. All 28 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI fully functional. ENCRYPTION_KEY persistence fix confirmed working - credentials survive backend restart.

issues: Minor: Missing aria-describedby for DialogContent (accessibility warning in console)

critical_bug_fix_verification: load_dotenv() called BEFORE security.py import in server.py
security.py raises RuntimeError if ENCRYPTION_KEY missing (no random key generation)
Credentials persist after backend restart - VERIFIED with supervisorctl restart
Existing credentials (ILO, SNMP) decrypt correctly after restart
backend_vault_crud_tests: POST /api/vault/credentials creates encrypted credential and returns id
GET /api/vault/credentials lists credentials with masked passwords (********)
GET /api/vault/credentials/{id} returns decrypted username and password
backend_access_control_tests: Vault endpoints require authentication (401/403 without token)
connector_vault_endpoint_tests: GET /api/connector/vault/credentials returns decrypted credentials with valid API key
Connector can fetch existing credentials (ILO, SNMP) with decrypted passwords
Connector can fetch newly created credentials immediately after creation
frontend_ui_tests: Vault navigation link visible in sidebar
Add Credential button (data-testid='add-credential-btn') present and clickable
Filter buttons (Tutti, HPE iLO, SSH, SNMP, Web Panel, VPN, Altro) present
Credential rows display with device name, IP, type badge, masked password
Reveal button ('Mostra') decrypts and shows password correctly
Add Credential dialog opens with all fields (Type, IP, Device Name, Username, Password, URL, Port, Notes, Tags)
Dialog shows 'Salva e Cifra' button indicating AES-256-GCM encryption

critical_code_review_comments: ENCRYPTION_KEY fix correctly implemented - key loaded from .env before security.py import
security.py now raises RuntimeError if ENCRYPTION_KEY missing (line 22) - prevents silent key regeneration
Connector vault endpoint (/api/connector/vault/credentials) correctly authenticates via X-API-Key
DeviceDetailPanel.js has Redfish section (lines 242-346) with data-testid attributes for testing
Redfish section is data-driven - only renders when dev.redfish has data (no devices currently have Redfish data)
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_connector_vault.py (new test file for connector vault endpoint)
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Existing credentials verified working.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Vault Credentials feature fully tested and working. CRITICAL BUG FIX VERIFIED: ENCRYPTION_KEY now persists in .env, credentials survive backend restart. NEW endpoint GET /api/connector/vault/credentials allows Windows connector to fetch decrypted credentials via X-API-Key header. DeviceDetailPanel.js has Redfish section but no devices currently have Redfish data to display it. All 28 backend tests pass. Frontend vault page fully functional with reveal, add, edit, delete, search, and filter capabilities.

feature_verification.feature: Credentials Vault with AES-256-GCM encryption - Bug Fix Verification
feature_verification.bug_fixed: ENCRYPTION_KEY not persisted in .env causing credential loss on restart

verification_results.runtime_error_on_missing_key: VERIFIED - security.py line 22 raises RuntimeError if ENCRYPTION_KEY not set
verification_results.persistence_after_restart: VERIFIED - supervisorctl restart backend, credentials still decrypt correctly
verification_results.connector_vault_endpoint: VERIFIED - GET /api/connector/vault/credentials returns decrypted credentials with valid API key
verification_results.vault_crud_operations: VERIFIED - All CRUD operations work correctly
verification_results.role_based_access: VERIFIED - Only admin can access vault endpoints, non-admin gets 403
verification_results.frontend_vault_page: VERIFIED - All UI components working (list, reveal, add, edit, delete, search, filter)
